code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent, complex sentence. Unknown predicates fall back to a generic phrasing.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping from predicates to phrase templates (subject omitted)
    _templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {}",
        "location": "is located in {}",
        "country": "is in {}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at an elevation of {} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies at an elevation of {} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies at an elevation of {} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {}",
        "owner": "is owned by {}",
        "largestCity": "has its largest city {}",
        "capital": "has its capital {}",
        "language": "has the official language {}",
        "officialLanguage": "has the official language {}",
        "leader": "has leader {}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {}",
        "leaderParty": "has a leader belonging to the {} party",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in the administrative arrondissement of {}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {}",
        "currency": "uses the {}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {}",
        "regionServed": "serves the {} region",
        "hubAirport": "{} is a hub airport for {}",
        "headquarter": "has its headquarters at {}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county of {}",
        "countySeat": "has its county seat {}",
        "chief": "has chief {}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over {}",
        "city": "is located in {}",
    }

    # Helper to build a phrase (subject omitted when possible)
    def _phrase(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        # Runway length variants (e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet)
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            return f"has a {idx} runway that is {o} feet long"
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            return f"has a {idx} runway that is {o} metres long"
        # Runway surface type variants
        if p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            return f"has a {idx} runway surface of {o}"
        # Runway number variants
        if p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            return f"has a {idx} runway number {o}"
        # Direct template lookup
        tmpl = _templates.get(p)
        if tmpl:
            # Special case for hubAirport where subject appears after object
            if p == "hubAirport":
                return tmpl.format(o, s)
            return tmpl.format(o)
        # Fallback
        return f"has {p} {o}"

    # Build phrases, grouping by subject where possible
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else ""
    phrases = [_phrase(t.subject, t.predicate, _clean(t.object)) for t in triples]

    # If there are multiple distinct subjects, keep each clause separate
    distinct_subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
    if len(distinct_subjects) > 1:
        # Simple fallback: concatenate full clauses
        clauses = [f"{t.subject} {_phrase(t.subject, t.predicate, _clean(t.object))}" for t in triples]
        sentence = "; ".join(clauses) + "."
    else:
        # One main subject – create a fluent single sentence
        if not phrases:
            return ""
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {phrases[0]}."
        else:
            # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last phrase
            body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {body}."
    # Capitalise first character
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
